hero.name: Byeongheon Jang
hero.title: Technical Product Manager
hero.subtitle:
I organize user problems into product language with technical context in mind,
and I care about service direction that can scale without losing clarity.
hero.mission: My portfolio is built on structural thinking from philosophy, coursework across AI, data, and business, and hands-on experience connecting planning with implementation.

identity.title: Identity & Product Philosophy
identity.text1: My background in Philosophy trained me to define the core structure of a problem before jumping to features. I tend to start from the direction a product should take and the underlying issue it should solve.
identity.text2: Coursework across AI, data, and business expanded that mindset into a more practical one. I try to consider service context, data flow, and operational risk together rather than treating them as separate concerns.
identity.text3:
"I treat AI as a layer for better decisions, not as a gimmick."
In practice, I have used user logs to identify where people get stuck and designed AI interactions to support better questions and better decisions rather than relying on automation for its own sake.

projects.title: Core Projects
projects.intro: I chose to focus on one representative project and show how problem framing and design intent came together in practice.

loggy.title: Loggy | Real-Time Log Analysis Collaboration Platform
loggy.summary: This project was designed to reduce slow debugging cycles by improving real-time log visibility and team collaboration.

metrics.2: Designed with 5,000+ logs per second in mind

loggy.problem: Developers were spending too much time manually reading large volumes of logs, which slowed root-cause analysis and debugging.
loggy.insight: The issue was not simply log volume. The deeper problem was the lack of visibility into the right context at the right moment.
loggy.architecture: I designed the experience around WebSocket-based streaming and a structured log pipeline so that review, filtering, and decision-making could happen in one continuous flow.
loggy.role: Product Owner & Frontend Lead | Problem definition, user flow design, frontend implementation, and real-time review experience
loggy.learning: This project strengthened my ability to translate a developer pain point into screen flow, requirements, and implementation discussions with the engineering team.

platform.title: Product Direction & Platform > Feature
platform.desc: I try not to get trapped in one-off feature expansion. I prefer starting from the structural problem a product should solve and the direction it should grow toward.

alignment.title: Market Insight & Roadmap Mindset
alignment.desc: I look at user pain, operating context, and stakeholder needs together when setting priorities. My goal is to connect near-term execution with a longer-term roadmap.

depth.title: Technical Depth as Leverage
depth.subtitle: Technical literacy matters to me not as a way to boast implementation, but as a way to reduce product risk and lower communication cost across teams.

state.title: State Transition & Data Integrity
state.desc: Understanding how state changes and data consistency work between client and server helps me make requirements more concrete and reduces avoidable misalignment during development.

realtime.title: System-level Risk Reduction
realtime.desc: When I understand real-time data flow and concurrency at a practical level, I can think earlier about the bottlenecks and instability that may appear later in operation.

ai.title: AI Strategy & Deployment
ai.subtitle: I see AI less as a flashy add-on and more as a design question about how to support better user decisions and better user behavior.

responsibility.title: Decision-Support & Human-in-the-loop
responsibility.desc: I prefer designing AI as a support layer that helps people think and decide more clearly, rather than as a substitute for every piece of judgment.

integration.title: Risk Containment Architecture
integration.desc: In one AI diary project, I used user logs to identify the blank-screen moment where users stalled and redesigned the experience with follow-up questions, which doubled the average diary length.

framework.title: Executive-Level Problem Solving Framework
framework.subtitle: I try to reframe problems structurally and build execution loops that can be validated early.

1.name: Define Structural Problem
1.desc: I start by looking past surface symptoms to find the deeper bottleneck and user friction underneath.

2.name: Analyze System-Level Risk
2.desc: I review technical constraints and operating risks together to understand what may grow into a larger issue later.

3.name: Design Scalable Model
3.desc: I try to think beyond the immediate fix and shape a model that can still hold as features and usage expand.

4.name: Validate with Minimal Structure
4.desc: I prefer validating the smallest meaningful structure first, then using actual response and data to decide the next step.

5.name: Expand with Platform Lens
5.desc: Once the logic is validated, I look at how that structure can extend into adjacent user needs and product contexts.

ssafy.title: Cross-Functional Collaboration & Platform Thinking
ssafy.desc1: I have been working through algorithms, web development, and AI service projects while gaining first-hand experience in technical decision-making.
ssafy.desc2: Across several projects, I have handled problem framing, role coordination, screen flow design, and collaboration with developers.
ssafy.desc3: Projects around real-time logs, AI interaction design, and hackathons have helped me connect planning with implementation.

marines.title: Decision Clarity Under Extreme Pressure
marines.desc1: I learned to make priorities and responsibility explicit even in high-pressure situations.
marines.desc2: During KMEP joint training, I supported communication as an interpreter and learned the value of clear explanation and coordination.

jaguar.title: Customer Experience System Operation
jaguar.desc1: I managed VIP customer response and vehicle flow with an end-to-end view of the on-site service experience.
jaguar.desc2: By identifying bottlenecks and improving movement and response procedures, I helped raise operational efficiency.

restaurant.title: Business Model & Revenue Structure Thinking
restaurant.desc1: I handled early-stage operations, customer response, and physical flow, which gave me a grounded sense of service operations.
restaurant.desc2: That experience helped me develop a practical instinct for balancing service quality with operational efficiency.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: CONTACT
contact.description: I am always open to conversations about new opportunities, product strategy, and AI-enabled service design.
